title: Pure CatBoost categorical specialist with ordered CTRs
summary: Your current best solutions handcraft ordinal/frequency encodings for Driver, Race, Compound, and Race_Year.
rationale: Your current best solutions handcraft ordinal/frequency encodings for Driver, Race, Compound, and Race_Year. CatBoost’s official guidance is to keep categoricals raw, avoid manual one-hot preprocessing, and rely on ordered categorical statistics and combinations. That is especially attractive here because Driver and Race identities are high-cardinality and interaction-heavy.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost specialist on mostly raw features plus a compact set of strong numeric tyre-window features. Pass categorical columns directly, preserve sequence order inside folds when generating OOF predictions, and let CatBoost learn Driver x Race x Compound interactions rather than manually frequency-encoding all of them.
expected_effect: Can recover signal from rare drivers/races and category combinations that linear frequency features flatten away.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race session and predictions are not truly OOF, ordered CTR benefits can be overstated; ranking mode is tempting but start with a plain probabilistic specialist first.
